Workout Splits
Start with the FitCheck go-to split, switch to PPL + Arms, or build your own page from scratch.
💪 Push Pull Legs (PPL) — 6 Days/Week
The gold standard for muscle building. Each muscle group gets trained twice per week with dedicated push (chest, shoulders, triceps), pull (back, biceps, forearms), and legs days. Perfect for beginners and intermediate lifters.
6 Days/Week Each Muscle 2×/Week Intermediate Friendly Hypertrophy Focus

📌 How the PPL Split Works

  • Day 1 (Push): Chest, shoulders, triceps
  • Day 2 (Pull): Back, biceps, forearms
  • Day 3 (Legs): Quads, hamstrings, calves
  • Day 4–6: Repeat the same pattern

🔑 Key Concepts for Beginners

  • 2×failure means: pick a weight where you fail (can't do another rep) at around 8–12 reps. Do 2 sets like that.
  • Progressive Overload: Every week, try to add a little more weight or one more rep. This is how you grow.
  • Rest between sets: 60–90 seconds for isolation exercises, 2–3 minutes for compound lifts.
  • Tip: Log your weights in the fields below each exercise so you can beat them next week!

🍽️ Basic Nutrition Tips

  • Eat 0.8–1g of protein per lb of bodyweight daily.
  • Eat in a slight calorie surplus to build muscle (+200–300 cal/day).
  • Stay hydrated — at least 3 litres of water per day.
FitCheck Go-To Split — Upper / Lower / Upper / Lower / Arms
This is the main split I would put beginners and early intermediates on: simple structure, enough volume to grow, and a clear arm day so the program feels complete.
5 Days/Week Upper 2x/Week Lower 2x/Week Beginner Friendly Arms Included

Weekly Structure

  • Day 1 (Upper A): Chest, shoulders, triceps, with a little back balance
  • Day 2 (Lower A): Quads, hamstrings, calves
  • Day 3 (Upper B): Back, biceps, rear delts, with pressing support
  • Day 4 (Lower B): RDLs, split squats, leg press, curls, calves
  • Day 5 (Arms & Abs): Dedicated arm and core work
  • Days 6-7: Rest, steps, stretching, or light cardio

How to Progress

  • Keep 1-2 reps in the tank on heavy compound lifts until your form is solid.
  • For isolation work, stop when your clean form breaks instead of swinging the weight.
  • When you hit the top of the rep range on every set, add a small amount of weight next time.
  • Log your weights and reps below each exercise so next week has a clear target.
  • Rest 2-3 minutes for big lifts and 60-90 seconds for isolation work.

Simple Nutrition

  • Eat protein with every meal and aim for roughly 0.8-1g per lb of bodyweight daily.
  • To build muscle, eat slightly above maintenance. To cut, keep protein high and reduce calories slowly.
  • Sleep matters. A split only works if recovery is there too.
Build Your Own Split
Use this page when the go-to split does not match your schedule. Add your own days, exercises, and sets, then track it like a normal program.
